messages.content:
Coaches also know that commitment is within a person. So a coach

a. must be motivated and trustworthy.

b. must be firm and fair

c. creates an environment where people will want to choose to be committed.

d. must seek to the welfare of others
c. creates an environment where people will want to choose to be committed.

The last part of the coaching model has to do with coaches
a. taking part in clinical activities where the coach reflects on his practices
b. knowing themselves and being able to manage themselves especially under pressure.
c. dealing with problematic people
d. inventing strategies to perfect their skills
b. knowing themselves and being able to manage themselves especially under pressure.

Achieving the results and building the commitment of the people you coach is termed
a. Performance

b. Goal setting

c. Self evaluation

d. Motivation
a. Performance

The understanding that ‘people work hardest when they know that what they do matters' is considered as …....... in coaching
a.
motivation

b.
trust

c.
competences

d.
recognition
b. trust

How does a coach build results and commitment.
a.
through a relationship with the person they coach

b.
through clinical supervision

c.
mentoring

d.
Rewards and punishment
a. through a relationship with the person they coach

How can continuing teachers to gain valuable insights about teaching and student learning to deeply understand their own developing practice?

a.
through counselling and follow up services

b.
by developing teaching portfolio

c.
through the payment of CPD allowance to teachers

d.
through carefully designed, facilitated observations and reflective follow-up sessions
d. through carefully designed, facilitated observations and reflective follow-up sessions

Enhancing growth and increasing skills in a given profession, while building a trusting relationship between a supervisor and a supervisee is part of …………………………………………………

a.
Induction

b.
Clinical supervision

c.
Reflective practice

d.
Professional development
b. Clinical supervision

Reflective practice is the part of the cycle where we extract learning.

a.
False

b.
True
b. True

All are ways record takings can be done in a classroom observation except


a.
specific verbatim

b.
manual view

c.
audio tapping

d.
general observation
b. manual view

One objective of the supervisory conference is


a.
to improve upon classroom infrastructure

b.
to evaluate learning outcomes and promote accountability in learning

c.
to help the teacher make more functional use of his own resources and therefore perform more effectively within the classroom.

d.
for the teacher to critically examine the learning of the pupils and use support system to address the learning gap
c. to help the teacher make more functional use of his own resources and therefore perform more effectively within the classroom.

Why is it important for both mentor and mentee or coach and coachee to reflect on their meetings themselves and take actions?

a.
to build confidence in learning

b.
to help devise strategies for improvements

c.
to minimise discovered errors

d.
to promote lesson evaluation
b. to help devise strategies for improvements

Mrs Rose Atinga, a Chemistry teacher took her class through some lessons on Titration. After the lesson, she took time to analyse her experiences and actions in order to improve and develop herself. What Mrs Rose Atinga can be considered as engaging in

a.
reflective practice

b.
induction

c.
remedial lesson

d.
mentoring and coaching of student
a. reflective practice
what is counseling in three sentences?

Counseling is a professional relationship where a trained counselor helps an individual explore their feelings, thoughts, and behaviors in a safe and supportive environment. It involves active listening, empathy, and guidance to help clients gain insight, make positive changes, and improve their overall well-being. Through counseling, individuals can explore and resolve personal challenges, develop coping strategies, and work towards personal growth and fulfillment.
